citation_and_locator: Wong, Anita Mei-Yin. 2023. Understanding Development and Disorder in Cantonese Using Language Sample Analysis. Routledge; pp. 55-56 and 127-129, pivotal-construction definition and examples
what_it_supports: Defines the pivot NP as object of V1 and subject of V2; says a small first-verb set is licensed; exact `我揀佢做班長`, `我唔俾佢睇電視`, and conversational `我哋叫佢一齊玩` profiles.
limit: A clinical/developmental coding framework and selected examples do not establish the exhaustive first-verb inventory, all control analyses, `ling6/sai2`, or omission rules.

citation_and_locator: Lai, Yik-Po, and Wai-Kit Pang. 2023. Rethinking the Description and Typology of Cantonese Causative–Resultative Constructions: A Dynamic Constructionist Lens. Languages 8(2):151; section 3.1, example 5
what_it_supports: Contains `叫佢讀書` and explicitly distinguishes that affirmative command/complement profile from the causative-resultative construction under study.
limit: The article studies broad causative-resultatives, not the complete lexical or pragmatic grammar of `叫 NP VP`.

citation_and_locator: Chin, Andy Chi-On. 2011. Grammaticalization of the Cantonese double-object verb bei2 in typological and areal perspectives; checked pp. 9-14
what_it_supports: Distinguishes lexical transfer, beneficiary, causative/permissive, and passive `俾` profiles and documents contextual ambiguity.
limit: Does not license non-`俾` pivotal verbs or provide a complete parser decision procedure.
